notes: Fey Faire · seven endings with full script text.  Each has: trigger conditions, subtitle text, opening cutscene beats, main script (with $variable substitution from boot questionnaire), closing image, credits sig, cross-Oneironautics unlocks.  Scripts are the runtime content · the actual on-screen text the game renders at each ending.  Variables in $BRACKETS pull from state (e.g. $LOST_PERSON, $CITY_YOU_LIVE_IN, $FAVORITE_SONG, $NAME).

schema_notes.trigger: state conditions that fire this ending · Court alignments, keepsakes, disposition, promise-count
schema_notes.beats: sequential cutscene beats · each is a script segment with a specific screen composition
schema_notes.variables: state-substituted variables that appear in the script text

endings.name: A ROSE
endings.subtitle: the seelie ending

beats.background: Talikan · at first light · a specific window opens outward
beats.text: You leave the Faire with a rose in your buttonhole.

The rose does not wilt.  You keep your name.  You keep your face.  You keep every friend you had.  You $NO_LONGER_REMEMBER_THE_SEASON that specific loss (from $LOST_PERSON) happened in.  You catch yourself humming a Midsummer song at odd moments.
Titania keeps her promise to you.  Ondine watches you leave through the Gate.  The rose blooms in your buttonhole for exactly one year.

beats.image: $PLAYER_NAME's front door in $CITY_YOU_LIVE_IN · a specific morning · the rose visible on the coat rack

endings.closing_image: the rose blooming in a specific still frame
cross_oneironautics_unlocks: seed a 'Titania blessing' keepsake in the SlowstockShelf compendium

endings.name: A RED CAP
endings.subtitle: the unseelie ending

beats.background: Talikan · at dusk · you leave via a specific back gate no other tourist knows
beats.text: You leave the Faire wearing a knitted red cap.

It fits well.  You keep the seasons.  You keep every friend you had.  Your NAME is different now.  Everyone who calls you the old name gets confused for a moment before you correct them.  You have to relearn to answer to a name you did not choose.

Oberon keeps his promise to you (whatever it was · you are trying to remember what it was · it will come back to you eventually · you think).  The Erlking passes your house at night and does not stop.

beats.image: you in a specific new coat with a specific red cap · looking in a specific bathroom mirror · adjusting the cap · your reflection is you with a specific new expression

endings.closing_image: the red cap on a specific hat rack
cross_oneironautics_unlocks: seed an 'Oberon promise' keepsake in the SlowstockShelf compendium

endings.name: A STAG'S ANTLER
endings.subtitle: the wildfey ending

beats.background: the Faire · at dawn · the Faire is packing up · trailers being hooked to trucks
beats.text: You leave the Faire holding a stag's antler wrapped in oiled paper.

You do not know how to explain it.  You keep everything.  But: you do not really leave.  You are AT the Faire when it packs up at dawn.  And you are on one of the trucks.  And you are there when it unpacks in another town seventeen years later.
You come back home older, and the town has changed, and everyone you loved has grown up or died.  You are 32 now.  Your mother, if she is alive, is 71.

beats.image: you at 32 · standing on the porch of your old childhood house · in a town you barely recognize · holding the antler

endings.closing_image: the antler on a mantle · specifically in a house that is not yours anymore
cross_oneironautics_unlocks: seed a 'Wildfey stag-antler' keepsake in the SlowstockShelf compendium
unlock the Faire's next-town ambient scene as a bonus BGM

endings.name: REFUSED THE FAIRE
endings.subtitle: you walked away

beats.background: the Faire's Parking Lot · at night · a specific streetlight flickering
beats.text: You walk home under a streetlight that flickers behind you.
You never know what you missed.
Someone at school asks the next Monday if you went to that carnival that was there over the weekend.

beats.speaker: $PLAYER_NAME
beats.text: No.
You say no.  You say no honestly.  You do not remember it.

beats.image: you at your school desk · Monday morning · looking down at your specific homework

endings.closing_image: a specific empty lot where the Faire had been · early morning light · fully mundane

endings.name: BECOME THE FAIRE
endings.subtitle: you take Cricket's seat

beats.text: Cricket has been at the Gate a very long time.  She would like to retire.

beats.speaker: Cricket
beats.text: You have understood.  We have nodded at each other for six nights.  I have selected you.  Do you consent?
You accept.  You take the ticket-taker's stool.  You bite the corner off tickets.
Seventeen years pass in one night.
A new teenager walks up.

beats.speaker: You
beats.text: You get six nights.
You mean it.

beats.image: you · specifically shrunken to cricket-scale · sitting on a stool on the ticket counter · in a specific new town · watching a specific new teenager approach

endings.closing_image: the specific Gate under a specific new streetlight in a specific new town
cross_oneironautics_unlocks: seed a 'Cricket has retired' keepsake in the SlowstockShelf compendium
unlock a specific new-ticket-taker cameo in future Oneironautics releases

endings.name: BRING BACK THE LOST
endings.subtitle: $LOST_PERSON comes home

beats.background: The Green · a specific clearing · dawn light through the canopy
beats.text: The Green Man does not speak often.  He speaks now.

beats.speaker: The Green Man
beats.text: There is one exchange I will make.
If $LOST_PERSON was Andrew, the Green Man asks for your most-recruited fey in trade.  You lose them permanently to Fairyland.

beats.if_no_returns_to: no_ending_fires · Night 6 continues without this ending

beats.text: $LOST_PERSON comes home in [$LOST_YEAR_IF_KNOWN | your specific past you cannot revisit | in the history of a place you weren't at].
The ripple travels forward.  On the drive home from the Faire your father tells you a story about $LOST_PERSON.  Except he doesn't die.  Because you gave a fey back.

beats.image: specifically rendered based on $LOST_PERSON · if Andrew (Camp Sweetgum 1976): a photograph of Andrew at 45, alive, on a beach with his own children · if a moved-away friend: their handwritten letter arriving in your mailbox · if a father who hits: a specific empty chair at Thanksgiving dinner in a house that is warmer for the emptiness

endings.credits_dedication: for the ones we could not save · and the specific one we did
cross_oneironautics_unlocks: specifically REWRITES cross-Oneironautics facts in other Oneironautics slowsticks · if Andrew · Pirate Summer's Wilson Ashe now has a specific 'Andrew wrote to me' dialogue tree · if a moved-away friend · Estuary 3's specific Manager Mode customer changes · if a hitting father · a specific Kwik Stop customer's demeanor changes

endings.name: YOU FORGET WHY YOU CAME
endings.subtitle: the Faire keeps you

beats.background: empty lot · Night 7 · morning · the Faire has left
beats.text: You are still at the Faire on Night 7.
The Faire has left already.
You are alone in an empty lot.  You do not know why you came.  You do not know your name.
(the boot-questionnaire name $PLAYER_NAME still displays in the UI · but the character in the epilogue does not use it)
The screen fades to black.
You (the player, not the character) know what that means now.

beats.image: the empty lot at Night 7 · a specific single cricket visible on a specific patch of dirt · she is watching

endings.closing_image: black screen · text 'THE FAIRE MOVED ON'
